name: Gobhi Paratha
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Finely chopped fresh ginger and root
⅛ tsp Ground hot red pepper
1 medium Cauliflower, trimmed and coarsely chopped
1 tbsp Finely chopped fresh hot green chili (CAUTION)
recipeInstructions:
Indian Flat bread with spiced cauliflower filling
DOUGH:
1. In a small bowl, combine whole-wheat flour and ¼ cup of ghee. Rub the flour and fat together until they look like flakes of coarse meal.
2. Pour 1 cup of water over the mixture all at once. Knead together vigorously and gather the dough into a compact ball.
3. If the dough crumbles, add up to 2 cups more water, ¼ cup at a time, until the particles adhere.
4. Place the dough on a lightly floured surface. Knead by folding it end-to-end, then pressing down and pushing forward with the heel of your hand. Repeat for 7 or 8 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
5. Gather the dough into a ball, cover with a kitchen towel, and let rest at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before rolling.
CAULIFLOWER FILLING:
6. Meanwhile, heat 2 tablespoons of ghee in a heavy skillet over high heat until a drop of water flicked in splutters instantly.
7. Stir in ginger, garlic, onions. Lower the heat to moderate and, stirring constantly, fry for 7 or 8 minutes, until onions are soft and golden brown. Watch carefully for any sign of burning and regulate the heat accordingly.
8. Stir in turmeric, red pepper, cauliflower, chili, cumin, and salt. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 10 minutes, or until cauliflower is tender but still slightly resistant to the bite.
9. Divide the filling into 9 equal portions. Divide the dough also into 9 portions and cover with a damp kitchen towel.
PARATHAS:
10. On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball of dough into a round about 5 inches in diameter.
11. Place a portion of the filling mixture in the center of the round and fold the edges over it, pressing together to enclose securely.
12. Pat the filled paratha flat with hands, then gently roll again to make a round about 5 inches in diameter.
13. Set each paratha aside, covered with a damp kitchen towel.
COOKING:
14. Heat an ungreased skillet or griddle until a drop of water flicked in splutters instantly.
15. Place one paratha in the pan and move it constantly with fingers or edge of a metal spatula. Cook for 1 minute.
16. Brush about a teaspoon of ghee on the top, turn over, cook for another 2 minutes, then brush another teaspoon of ghee and cook for 1 more minute.
17. Continue to cook, turning frequently until brown on both sides.
18. Serve parathas warm as a mealtime bread. Traditionally accompanied by plain yogurt or raita. Parathas may be cooked ahead and reheated in a hot skillet for about 1 minute on each side.
Makes nine 5-inch breads.
